title: Closed ambient-context surface — every environment variable the spine may read, tied to this JSON record

authority.scope_source: clotho/memory/CONTRACTS/package-roots.json (AM-40 — the scan scope is itself a known JSON quantity)
authority.security_model: CLAUDE.md#security (secrets live in env / OS registry only, never the repo)

note: Runs are context-light because the spine's entire ambient surface is CLOSED: every process.env read across the AM-40 package roots must be a name in this contract, match a declared dynamic pattern, or occur in an enumerated file with a recorded justification. The verifier scans the source and fails on any read outside this record — and bidirectionally: a contract entry no source uses is also a failure (no phantom surface).

patterns.why: per-seat HMAC secrets, one per council seat (CLAUDE/AGY/CODEX/... + TESTER in tests); the seat set varies so the name is a derived, prefixed quantity
per-role reasoning-effort override; role names are data, so the name is a derived, prefixed quantity

dynamic_read_files.why: loadWin32Env fills MISSING env vars from the HKCU registry (generic name loop; never overwrites an existing var) and requireEnv(name) is an indirection whose call sites pass the static names above
registry-loader test fixture (creates/expands/deletes throwaway test keys)
generates child-process fixture code (template literals) exercising the registry loader with throwaway names

whole_env_files.why: spawning an MCP server child: inherits the parent env then applies explicit overrides — the child's surface is still bounded by this contract
scrubbedEnv is a SANITIZER: it takes the env in order to REMOVE secrets before handing it to the sandboxed verifier child — context-reducing, not context-consuming
copies the env and NEUTRALIZES every repository-redirecting GIT_* var so git inspects only the intended repo — hermetic/context-reducing
test for the neutralized-copy behavior above (controlled copies of the env in fixtures)
comment prose discussing the env plus generated fixture code that manipulates it wholesale in a throwaway child
snapshots and restores the whole env around the registry-loader fixture
snapshots and restores the whole env around the registry-loader fixture

why: A future model must be able to answer 'what outside the repo can influence this code?' from one record. Everything ambient is here: API keys, model overrides, HMAC secret patterns, plugin locations, and the enumerated files that handle the env wholesale — each of those either bounds, scrubs, or neutralizes it.
change_rule: Reading a NEW env var requires adding it here in the same change (the scanner fails otherwise) — which is the point: ambient context cannot grow silently. Removal of a read requires removing the entry (the phantom check fails otherwise).

oracle.source_of_truth: every .mjs file under the AM-40 package roots
oracle.contract_equals_code: docs/institutional-memory/verify-contracts.mjs (scans all spine sources: every read classified and matched against this record, bidirectionally — no unlisted reads, no phantom entries, no stale file allowances)
